title: Mirror registry ignored
body:
Hello,

I am trying to set up a local mirror of docker.io on Centos 7 with a local registry running on localhost:5000. Let's say I pull an image a first time from docker.io with `podman pull docker.io/busybox`. Now if the mirror is set up correctly, I would expect that, when I remove this image and pull it a second time with the same command, it would be pulled from the local registry at localhost:5000. However, this is not what happens. It keeps pulling from docker.io and nothing happens in my local registry:

```
# curl localhost:5000/v2/_catalog
{"repositories":[]}
```

This is my `/etc/containers/registries.conf` file:
```
[[registry]]
location="docker.io"
[[registry.mirror]]
location="localhost:5000"
insecure=true
```

What would be the problem? 

```
# podman --version
podman version 1.6.4
# podman info --debug
debug:
  compiler: gc
  git commit: ""
  go version: go1.12.12
  podman version: 1.6.4
host:
  BuildahVersion: 1.12.0-dev
  CgroupVersion: v1
  Conmon:
    package: conmon-2.0.8-1.el7.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/conmon
    version: 'conmon version 2.0.8, commit: f85c8b1ce77b73bcd48b2d802396321217008762'
  Distribution:
    distribution: '"centos"'
    version: "7"
  MemFree: 2003165184
  MemTotal: 3973255168
  OCIRuntime:
    name: runc
    package: runc-1.0.0-69.rc10.el7_9.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/runc
    version: 'runc version spec: 1.0.1-dev'
  SwapFree: 0
  SwapTotal: 0
  arch: amd64
  cpus: 2
  eventlogger: journald
  hostname: some-registry
  kernel: 3.10.0-1160.66.1.el7.x86_64
  os: linux
  rootless: false
  uptime: 1h 14m 44.67s (Approximately 0.04 days)
registries:
  blocked: null
  insecure: null
  search: null
store:
  ConfigFile: /etc/containers/storage.conf
  ContainerStore:
    number: 2
  GraphDriverName: overlay
  GraphOptions: {}
  GraphRoot: /var/lib/containers/storage
  GraphStatus:
    Backing Filesystem: xfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "true"
    Supports d_type: "true"
    Using metacopy: "false"
  ImageStore:
    number: 3
  RunRoot: /var/run/containers/storage
  VolumePath: /var/lib/containers/storage/volumes

```

Thanks for reaching out, @maxclac!

Thanks, that helped!

> DEBU[0000] Ping https://localhost:5000/v2/ err Get https://localhost:5000/v2/: http: server gave HTTP response to HTTPS client (&url.Error{Op:"Get", URL:"https://localhost:5000/v2/", Err:(*errors.errorString)(0xc000396020)})

It looks like the local registry is running without TLS etc.  You can set it to insecure in the registries.conf via `insecure=true`.  Once that's set, the debug logs should indicate that only registry:5000 is used.
Thanks @vrothberg ! However, as I posted before, I already set the registry to insecure in my registries.conf.

Does it work when you set the following?
```
[[registry]]
location="localhost:5000"
insecure=true
```
Yes, even if obviously I can just pull from the local registry.

I can reproduce the issue on CentOS 7.9 but am unable to find a corresponding bug report since 1.6.4.   It's definitely working in more recent versions of Podman.

Any chance you can upgrade to a newer version?

If you want to see this fixed in 1.6.4, please open a bug in the Red Hat Bugzilla.  Upstream does not support 1.6.4 anymore.
Thanks a lot!
This is not likely to be back ported BTW,  Best option is to move to RHEL8.
I don't have access to RHEL8. I am trying on Centos 8 Stream with Podman 4.0.2 and it is still not working. Should I open a new ticket?

I should have taken more time to read the logs:
```
DEBU[0000] Looking for TLS certificates and private keys in /etc/docker/certs.d/localhost:5000 
DEBU[0000] GET https://localhost:5000/v2/               
DEBU[0000] Ping https://localhost:5000/v2/ err Get https://localhost:5000/v2/: http: server gave HTTP response to HTTPS client (&url.Error{Op:"Get", URL:"https://localhost:5000/v2/", Err:(*errors.errorString)(0xc000396020)}) 
DEBU[0000] GET http://localhost:5000/v2/                
DEBU[0000] Ping http://localhost:5000/v2/ status 200    
DEBU[0000] GET http://localhost:5000/v2/library/busybox/manifests/latest 
```

The registry is contacted correctly (i.e., the `insecure` setting works). Even if configured as insecure, Podman tries https first but I should have continued reading the logs.

My conclusion is that the mirror is not correctly configured as pull-through cache.  I just spun up a fresh CentOS 8 Stream VM, configured registries.conf as described.  I ran the mirror as follows: `podman run --rm -p 5000:5000 -e REGISTRY_PROXY_REMOTEURL="https://registry-1.docker.io" registry`
Thanks! The environment variable was what I was missing.

> Thanks! The environment variable was what I was missing.

Excellent, glad we could resolve the issue.

Hi! I would like to come back to this issue, because I am not sure it is quite solved. The image taken from docker.io is indeed pulled into the local registry but, when I try to pull again, the image is pulled again from docker.io. There is apparently no attempt to look into the local registry first.
I would like to reactivate this issue because it is actually not resolved. Am I the only one trying to do this?

Sure! Sorry for missing your earlier comment.

Can you share debug logs (and registries.conf) etc. to support your observations?

`registries.conf`:
```conf
unqualified-search-registries = ["docker.io"]

[[registry]]
location = "docker.io"
insecure = true

[[registry.mirror]]
location = "localhost:5000"
insecure = true
short-name-mode = "permissive"

```

Sorry for not going back to you earlier, @maxclac.

Your config is invalid, see
> DEBU[0000] Failed to decode keys ["registry.mirror.short-name-mode"] from "/etc/containers/registries.conf" 

The `short-name-mode` will be interpreted as an attribute of a `registry.mirror`.  It's just/unfortunately how TOML works.  Moving it up as shown below should fix your issue:
```TOML
unqualified-search-registries = ["docker.io"]
short-name-mode = "permissive"               
                                             
[[registry]]                                 
location = "docker.io"                       
insecure = true                              
                                             
[[registry.mirror]]                          
location = "localhost:5000"                  
insecure = true                              
```

The reason for not erroring out is forward/backward compatibility.  If we add a new field to `registries.conf` and not all clients are updated at the same time, we want old clients to continue work and not error out with "I do not know 'new_field'".  An error log would be equally annoying, so we opted for a debug log.

Thank you. This is still not working.

Could the following error message indicate what is wrong?
```
DEBU[0000] Ping https://localhost:5000/v2/ err Get "https://localhost:5000/v2/": http: server gave HTTP response to HTTPS client (&url.Error{Op:"Get", URL:"https://localhost:5000/v2/", Err:(*errors.errorString)(0xc000433460)}) 
```

Podman will always try `https` first.  With `insecure=true` it will fallback to using `http`, so you should see something like below in your logs:
```
DEBU[0000] GET https://localhost:5000/v2/                                                                                                                                                                                         
DEBU[0000] Ping https://localhost:5000/v2/ err Get "https://localhost:5000/v2/": http: server gave HTTP response to HTTPS client (&url.Error{Op:"Get", URL:"https://localhost:5000/v2/", Err:(*errors.errorString)(0xc00044f990)})
DEBU[0000] GET http://localhost:5000/v2/                                                                                                                                                                                          
DEBU[0000] Ping http://localhost:5000/v2/ status 200                                                                                                                                                                              
```
Yes, that's right. Something else is wrong, then.
Can you share what makes you deduce it doesn't work?  Happy to help debug.

What I expect from a mirror registry is, when I first pull an image from docker.io, it is automatically pushed to the mirror registry. When I subsequently do `podman pull`, it should pull from the local mirror registry. In my case, this is not happening and `podman pull` always pulls from docker.io.

If you see in the debug logs Podman contacting the mirror fist, Podman works as expected.  Whether the image is on the specified mirror is really up to this mirror.  Podman won't push the image to mirror.

But you will get the desired behavior by configuring the mirror to act as a pull-through cache.  The following command, for instance, configures the `registry:2` mirror to act as pull-through cache for quay.io:
`podman run -d -p 5000:5000 -e REGISTRY_PROXY_REMOTEURL="https://quay.io" registry:2`
It's still not working. Do I need to change `registries.conf`?
Also, I cannot find any documentation online about mirrors acting as a pull-through cache with Podman. Is it such a rare use case?

> It's still not working. Do I need to change `registries.conf`?

You need to configure `registries.conf` like that:
```TOML
[[registry]]                                 
location = "docker.io"                                                   
                                             
[[registry.mirror]]                          
location = "localhost:5000"                  
insecure = true  
```

And run the mirror like that: `podman run -d -p 5000:5000 -e REGISTRY_PROXY_REMOTEURL="https://quay.io" registry:2`

> Also, I cannot find any documentation online about mirrors acting as a pull-through cache with Podman. Is it such a rare use case?

I think you had a specific expectation of Podman and mirrors.  As mentioned above, Podman will not push to a mirror but only pull from it.  `man containers-registries.conf` goes into the details of the behavior (i.e., trying the mirrors in the specified order and as a last attempt the main registry).

When it comes to the behavior of `registry:2`, that is outside the control of Podman.  The details about that are a bit hidden (see https://docs.docker.com/registry/recipes/mirror/#run-a-registry-as-a-pull-through-cache).

But I'll take your feedback seriously and will sit down to write a blog post about this very example.

Thank you for this! Something is a bit strange: in `registries.conf`, the main registry is `docker.io`, but in your podman command, you use `quay.io` as proxy. Is it on purpose? Should this not be all `docker.io`?

I tried again with what you suggested and it is still not working.

Apologies, yes, for it to act as a pull-through cache for Docker Hub, you need to configure it to `https://registry-1.docker.io` (see https://docs.docker.com/registry/recipes/mirror/#run-a-registry-as-a-pull-through-cache).
Now it works! Thank you so much @vrothberg !
